criteria_results.criterion: JSDoc header documents --continue + -c as supported flags + explains unconditional-install + --tools-re-derivation rationale (constraint preservation requires re-derivation every invocation because pi does not persist per-session tool restrictions)
criteria_results.evidence: scripts/launch-constrained-pi.sh:40 declares 'Passthrough flags: --continue / -c — pi's canonical resume-last-session flag, honored as...' + follows with the rationale text. Verified post-edit. No canonical_id citations per FGAP-130 Pattern A.

criteria_results.criterion: Adversarial probe (fresh-context Explore) — skipped: header-only change to a bash script + the runtime demo extension provides verification at the script's exec boundary; a fresh-context probe would not surface additional false-pass scenarios beyond what the runtime demo already attests
criteria_results.evidence: Plan Phase 4 status cascade explicitly marks adversarial-probe skipped for this single-file header-only change.

evidence: FGAP-137 (filed at commit 1d3f0a6) closed via commit dcdcae7 (header-only edit + runtime-demo extension; install + composite-discovery + --tools allowlist + symmetry verification all unchanged per user direction that install stays unconditional). --continue / -c now first-class documented passthrough flags. Mechanical correctness was already in place (script's ARGS catch-all routes --continue to pi at exec); this arc adds the documentation contract + runtime-demo assertion that the contract holds. Verified empirically via pi-coding-agent recon that pi does NOT persist --tools per-session, making the script's existing every-invocation --tools re-derivation REQUIRED on --continue too (skipping it would silently unconstrain the resumed orchestrator). No release; no push; tree clean modulo pre-existing hedge.patterns.json mod.
